section.heading: Compacts

content.p: The Secretary shall negotiate and enter into a written compact with each Indian Tribe participating in self-governance in a manner consistent with the trust responsibility of the Federal Government, treaty obligations, and the government-to-government relationship between Indian Tribes and the United States.

paragraph.content: specify and affirm the general terms of the government-to-government relationship between the Indian Tribe and the Secretary; and
include such terms as the parties intend shall control during the term of the compact.

content.p: A compact under subsection (a) may be amended only by agreement of the parties.

subsection.heading: Effective date
subsection.chapeau: The effective date of a compact under subsection (a) shall be—

paragraph.content: the date of the execution of the compact by the parties; or
such date as is mutually agreed upon by the parties.

subsection.heading: Duration
subsection.chapeau: A compact under subsection (a) shall remain in effect—

paragraph.content: for so long as permitted by Federal law; or
until termination by written agreement, retrocession, or reassumption.

subsection.heading: Existing compacts
subsection.chapeau: An Indian Tribe participating in self-governance under this subchapter, as in effect on October 21, 2020, shall have the option at any time after that date—

paragraph.content: to retain its negotiated compact (in whole or in part) to the extent that the provisions of the compact are not directly contrary to any express provision of this subchapter; or
to negotiate a new compact in a manner consistent with this subchapter.

section.sourceCredit: (Pub. L. 93–638, title IV, § 404, as added Pub. L. 116–180, title I, § 101(e), Oct. 21, 2020, 134 Stat. 864.)

note.heading: Prior Provisions
note.p: A prior section 5364, Pub. L. 93–638, title IV, § 404, as added Pub. L. 103–413, title II, § 204, Oct. 25, 1994, 108 Stat. 4275, related to identification of funds in annual budget request, prior to repeal by Pub. L. 116–180, title I, § 101(e), Oct. 21, 2020, 134 Stat. 864. See section 5371 of this title. Such section was formerly classified to section 458dd of this title prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as section 5364.
